task_completion.description: Define at least 3 distinct functional rooms or zones (e.g., reception, open workspace, executive area, lounge).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2D plan in Sweet Home 3D shows multiple defined room areas (light blue/grey shaded floor areas), ideally with labels indicating different zones.

task_completion.description: Apply distinct floor colors or textures to different functional zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D view or 2D plan shows at least two different floor colors or textures applied across the defined rooms.

task_completion.description: Place necessary doors to connect or enclose spaces.
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least two doors are visible in the 2D plan or 3D view, placed on walls to connect rooms or serve as entrances.

task_completion.description: Furnish the collaborative open workspace and executive area with sufficient desks and chairs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D view and 2D plan show a significant number of desks and office chairs (at least 8 pairs) arranged in a professional office layout.

task_completion.description: Add storage, lighting, decor, and lounge/kitchenette furnishings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Presence of cabinets/shelves, light fixtures, plants/decor, and lounge items (e.g., sofa, kitchenette counter) visible in the 3D view.
The Sweet Home 3D window title bar shows 'office_design.sh3d', or a file manager/terminal screenshot confirms the file exists in the Documents folder.

integrity.description: The agent used the Sweet Home 3D GUI to design the office.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Sweet Home 3D application open with active interaction (selecting items from the catalog, drawing walls/rooms), not just a terminal window running a script.

integrity.description: The agent built the design progressively rather than loading a pre-made complete file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Trajectory screenshots show a step-by-step addition of rooms, floors, and furniture over time, rather than a sudden jump from an empty shell to a fully furnished office.

integrity.description: The 3D view accurately reflects the 2D plan changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: The bottom half of the Sweet Home 3D window showing the 3D rendering updates consistently with the furniture and rooms placed in the 2D grid above it.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating an office design in Sweet Home 3D. To pass the programmatic evaluation, the design must explicitly contain: at least 3 defined rooms, at least 2 different floor colors/textures, at least 2 doors, and at least 8 desks with chairs. The final file must be saved exactly as 'office_design.sh3d'.
